objective: Build the background worker polling loop that claims queued jobs from the durable job store and executes them through existing track or workflow execution paths.

background_worker_module: companion/jobs/worker.js — polling worker with start(), stop(), getStatus()
setInterval polling every 5 seconds (configurable), single concurrency (isProcessing flag)
Claims, starts, executes, and completes/fails/retries jobs through the durable job store
Retry logic: retryable errors with remaining attempts re-queue the job; non-retryable or exhausted attempts leave the job in failed
console.log lifecycle events (claimed, started, completed, failed, retried)
server_integration: companion/server.js — import createBackgroundWorker, initialize with durableJobStore and execution callbacks
Execution callbacks wrap runTrack (track jobs) and buildRunPlan+executeRunPlan (workflow jobs)
Worker starts automatically inside server.listen() callback after printStartupStatus()
tests: scripts/test-jobs-worker.js (44/44 — covers track completion, workflow completion, failure+retry, maxAttempts exhaustion, non-retryable error, single concurrency, skip-poll-while-processing, multi-job polling, getStatus, start/stop lifecycle)

results: Worker starts when the server starts, stops when stop() is called, reports status via getStatus()
Queued track-type job is claimed, executed via runTrack callback, marked completed with result
Queued workflow-type job is claimed, executed via runWorkflow callback, marked completed with result
Retryable failure with remaining attempts re-queues the job (failed → queued via retryJob)
Max attempts exhausted leaves job in failed status
Error with retryable: false leaves job in failed status
Worker processes only one job at a time; polling skips while processing
Wired into server.js — worker starts after server listens
node scripts/test-jobs-worker.js passes all test cases (44/44)
Existing tests still pass: node scripts/test-jobs-api.js (64/64), node scripts/test-durable-job-store.js (117/117)

passed: Worker starts when the server starts, stops when stop() is called, and reports status via getStatus()
A queued track-type job is claimed, executed via the track callback, and marked completed with the result
A queued workflow-type job is claimed, executed via the workflow callback, and marked completed with the result
When execution fails with a retryable error and attempts remain, the job is re-queued (failed → queued via retryJob)
When execution fails and max attempts are exhausted, the job stays in failed status
When execution fails with retryable: false, the job stays in failed status
Worker processes only one job at a time; polling skips while a job is being processed
Wired into server.js — the worker starts automatically after the server listens
node scripts/test-jobs-worker.js passes all test cases (44/44)
Existing tests still pass: node scripts/test-jobs-api.js (64/64), node scripts/test-durable-job-store.js (117/117), node scripts/contract-test.js
No changes to existing response envelope shapes or store methods

scope_notes: Cancel, retry, or mutation endpoints from the worker side not implemented
Operator console UI (HTML/CSS/JS dashboard) not included
companion/core/durable-job-store.js and its schema not modified
No cross-process workers, worker pools, or separate Node.js processes
stop_conditions_honored: No modifications to existing /tracks/run, /workflows/run, /tasks/run, or /analyze endpoints or their response envelopes
No modification to companion/core/durable-job-store.js or its schema
No modification to existing /jobs API endpoints
last_fresh_validation: 2026-07-12 (background worker polling loop implemented and tested — 44/44)
next_milestones: Human-gate endpoints (POST /jobs/:id/review, paused_review transitions); cancel/retry/mutation API endpoints
